metadata.title: Claude Cowork, Set Up — Skills and Projects, Not Folders
metadata.color_semantics: claude: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one earned accent per beat; mono for the composer command + skill file only
metadata.greeting_note: hello lexicon: Māori (two words — Bear's budget allows it). Wagwan check: charsum('cowork-setup') % 10 == 7, not 0.
metadata.source: Ruben Hassid, 'Learn 80% of Claude Cowork in 20 minutes' (Substack, Jul 2026) — his revised setup: forget files/folders, use Skills + Projects.
metadata.note: NEW claude-explainer reel teaching Cowork setup in the Claude cut. LAWS: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk cold open, never a brand card (type-on). ASK->RESULT: every generated result is preceded by the real Claude prompt that made it (B05 slash-command -> B06 the skill file). ILLUSTRATE LAW: the Claude UI appears only where the UI is the subject (asks, the skill file, artifact/verdict pages, handoff, outro); the three real-app moments are screenshot slots. SPARK-LINE LAW: every inner beat pairs the terracotta spark with one short serif line; typing appears in B00 and the handoff only. VERDICT as a one-page ClaudeWindow artifact. HANDOFF second-to-last (greeting 'Your turn.'). OUTRO restates the title. GATE P before any audio spend (ElevenLabs = paid — swap brand to 'claude-liam' + engine 'kokoro' voice am_onyx for a free previz). SCREENSHOT SLOTS (Bear fills): media/B01.png (the Cowork tab in the desktop app, mid-plan), media/B02.png (the old folder+files setup), media/B07.png (a shared Project). FAITHFUL to Ruben; honest nuance kept (folders are fine solo, they break on a team).
metadata.description_blurb: The creator who taught a million people to run Cowork with files and folders just told everyone to stop. Here's the setup that replaces it — Skills and Projects, nothing else — and why folders break the moment a team shares them.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Cowork isn't one Claude; a single prompt plans, then spins up parallel Claudes that can run for thirty minutes. The old folder setup breaks because folders leak stale outputs into fresh work and about-me files rot from neglect. A Skill is a capability you call: a name, a description of when to use it, and instructions underneath. A Project is a place you go: files and instructions stay loaded, memory persists across sessions, and a team shares one.

artifactLines: Cowork isn't one Claude; a single prompt plans, then spins up parallel Claudes that can run for thirty minutes.
The old folder setup breaks because folders leak stale outputs into fresh work and about-me files rot from neglect.
A Skill is a capability you call: a name, a description of when to use it, and instructions underneath.
A Project is a place you go: files and instructions stay loaded, memory persists across sessions, and a team shares one.

beats.narration_text: The claim that folders leak and files rot is the part I want to pressure-test before switching my team over. Say I currently keep an about-me file and an outputs folder: (1) rewrite what my about-me file contains into a Skill's name-plus-description-plus-instructions shape, (2) show what stays in a Project instead so it persists across sessions and my team shares it, and (3) give me one concrete test that proves Cowork stopped peeking at stale outputs once I've moved.

props.command: The claim that folders leak and files rot is the part I want to pressure-test before switching my team over. Say I currently keep an about-me file and an outputs folder: (1) rewrite what my about-me file contains into a Skill's name-plus-description-plus-instructions shape, (2) show what stays in a Project instead so it persists across sessions and my team shares it, and (3) give me one concrete test that proves Cowork stopped peeking at stale outputs once I've moved.

beats.narration_text: First, what Cowork even is. It lives in the desktop app, in the tab between Chat and Code. And it's not one Claude — it's many. You ask once; it plans, then spins up other Claudes to search, read, and build in parallel. One prompt can run for thirty minutes and hand you a finished thing.

shot.note: SLOT — Bear fills: screenshot of the Cowork tab in the desktop app mid-plan (the task list / parallel subagents visible). Cream frame, terracotta ring on the 'Cowork' tab. Falls back to a slate if empty.

beats.narration_text: Here's the setup everyone learned first — mine included. A folder on your computer: about-me files so Claude knows you, output files for its work. You pick the folder when a session starts. It's a good idea. It's also where the trouble begins.

shot.note: SLOT — Bear fills: screenshot of the old folder+files setup (a Cowork folder with about-me/ and output/ files, or the folder picker at session start). Falls back to a slate if empty.

beats.narration_text: Two things break it. Folders leak — tell Cowork to ignore your output folder and it still peeks, and stale results poison the fresh work. And files rot — nobody maintains an about-me file forever; it starts helpful and becomes a chore. Solo, you can survive both. Hand the folder to a team and it falls apart.

artifactLines: Folders leak — old outputs sneak into context and poison the new answer.
Files rot — the about-me file needs upkeep nobody has time for.
Fine for one person. Breaks the moment a team shares it.

beats.narration_text: The replacement is two things, and nothing else. A Skill is a capability you call. A Project is a place you go. Learn the difference and you've learned the setup.

artifactLines: Skill = a capability you call — a slash command, from inside any chat.
Project = a place you go — files and instructions stay loaded, and it remembers.
No about-me files to maintain. No output folders to leak.

beats.narration_text: Start with Skills. You call one right in the message box, with a slash — like this. It's not tied to a folder or a project; it travels into any chat. And because a skill is just instructions in a file, you can carry it anywhere — even paste it into another assistant.

props.command: /linkedin-post turn today's launch notes into a post in my voice

beats.narration_text: And here's the skill it just called — a plain file. A name, a description that tells Claude when to reach for it, and the instructions underneath. That's the whole trick: you write the judgment down once, and every future call inherits it.

props.title: linkedin-post/SKILL.md — a skill is just a file
props.code:
---
name: linkedin-post
description: >
  Turn notes or a link into a LinkedIn post in my
  voice. Use when I type /linkedin-post or ask for
  a LinkedIn draft.
---

# Voice: first person, one idea, a hook up top.
# Ban: hashtags, emoji, 'in today's world'.
# End on a question that invites a reply.
props.sparkLine: A skill is judgment, written once.

beats.narration_text: Then Projects. A project is a place you go, not a file you carry. Your files and instructions stay loaded, it remembers across sessions, and you share the one place with your team. This is how the enterprise rooms run it — everyone working in the same Project, calling the same Skills.

shot.note: SLOT — Bear fills: screenshot of a shared Project (left-menu Projects, files + instructions panel, share/collaborators visible). Terracotta ring on the share control. Falls back to a slate if empty.
shot.sparkLine: A project is a place you go.

beats.narration_text: The setup, on one page. Solo, folders are still fine — keep them. For a team, drop them: Skills for the capabilities you repeat, one shared Project for the place you all work. Pick Opus for hard tasks — or Fable when it's there. Then block twenty minutes this week and build your first Skill. That's it.

artifactLines: Solo → folders are fine. Team → drop them.
Skills = the capabilities you repeat, called by slash, portable anywhere.
Projects = one shared, persistent place your team works from.
Model: Opus for hard tasks (Fable when available). Block 20 min, build one Skill.
props.sparkLine: Set it up in twenty minutes.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Don't start from a blank folder — start from your most-repeated task. Paste this, and Claude turns it into your first Skill.

props.command: /skill-creator take the task I redo most each week and turn it into a Skill, then tell me how to load it into a shared Project for my team.

beats.narration_text: Claude Cowork, Set Up — Skills and Projects, Not Folders

props.title: Claude Cowork, Set Up — Skills and Projects, Not Folders
props.subline: Skills to call · Projects to share · build one this week
